---
title: "proteomics_cleaning"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r}
# Proteomics raw data is not available on this git hub due to not having permission to share. The paths for reading in the data are from the raw data files on my local directory that I have been granted access to. For permission to access thsi data, please refer to my README file.


library(tidyverse)


prot_path_cellular  <- "proteomics/Singh-Cellular-20713-4-V2/tables/Table-19.txt"
prot_path_soluble <- "proteomics/Singh-Soluble-20747-7/tables/Table-17.txt"

prot_raw_soluble <- read_tsv(prot_path_soluble, show_col_types = FALSE)
prot_raw_cellular <- read_tsv(prot_path_cellular, show_col_types = FALSE)

meta_cols <- c(
  "PG.ProteinGroups", "PG.Genes",
  "PG.ProteinDescriptions", "PG.UniProtIds", "PG.FastaFiles",
  "PG.Database", "PG.OrganismId", "PG.MolecularWeight",
  "PG.NrOfPrecursorsIdentified (Experiment-wide)",
  "GOMF name", "GOBP slim name", "KEGG name", "GOCC slim name"
)

# Define cleaning function to avoid repetition
clean_proteomics <- function(prot_raw, output_name) {
  
  # Per-sample log2 abundance columns
  log2_cols <- grep("\\.Quantity\\.log2$", names(prot_raw), value = TRUE)
  
  # Statistical result columns
  stat_cols <- grep(
    "^(p\\.value|adj\\.p\\.value|log2\\.fold\\.change|is\\.significant|t\\.statistic)\\.",
    names(prot_raw), value = TRUE
  )
  
  # Per-sample precursor count columns
  precursor_quant_cols <- grep("\\.NrOfPrecursorsUsedForQuantification$",
                               names(prot_raw), value = TRUE)
  
  prot_clean <- prot_raw %>%
    select(all_of(c(meta_cols, log2_cols, stat_cols))) %>%
    
    mutate(
      gene_symbol         = str_split(PG.Genes, ";", simplify = TRUE)[, 1],
      gene_symbol         = na_if(str_trim(gene_symbol), ""),
      uniprot_id          = str_split(PG.UniProtIds, ";", simplify = TRUE)[, 1],
      protein_description = str_split(PG.ProteinDescriptions, ";", simplify = TRUE)[, 1],
      mol_weight_da       = as.numeric(
                              str_split(PG.MolecularWeight, ";", simplify = TRUE)[, 1]
                            )
    ) %>%
    
    # Convert NaN -> NA
    mutate(across(all_of(log2_cols), ~ na_if(., NaN))) %>%
    
    # Per-row QC metrics
    mutate(
      n_samples_detected   = rowSums(!is.na(across(all_of(log2_cols)))),
      pct_samples_detected = round(100 * n_samples_detected / length(log2_cols), 1),
      
      median_precursors_used = if (length(precursor_quant_cols) > 0)
        apply(prot_raw[, precursor_quant_cols], 1,
              function(x) median(x[x > 0], na.rm = TRUE))
      else NA_real_
    ) %>%
    
    # Filter for reliability
    filter(is.na(median_precursors_used) | median_precursors_used >= 2) %>%
    
    # Per-time point mean log2 abundance
    {
      tp <- c("D14", "D21", "M03", "M06", "M12", "M18")
      df <- .
      for (t in tp) {
        cols_t <- grep(paste0("^", t, "\\..*\\.Quantity\\.log2$"),
                       names(df), value = TRUE)
        if (length(cols_t) > 0) {
          df[[paste0("mean_log2_", t)]] <- rowMeans(df[, cols_t], na.rm = TRUE)
        }
      }
      df
    } %>%
    
    rename(
      protein_groups        = PG.ProteinGroups,
      n_precursors_exp      = `PG.NrOfPrecursorsIdentified (Experiment-wide)`,
      go_molecular_function = `GOMF name`,
      go_biological_process = `GOBP slim name`,
      go_cellular_component = `GOCC slim name`,
      kegg_pathway          = `KEGG name`
    ) %>%
    
    select(
      # Identifiers
      gene_symbol, uniprot_id, protein_groups, protein_description,
      mol_weight_da, n_precursors_exp,
      # QC
      n_samples_detected, pct_samples_detected,
      median_precursors_used,
      # Per-timepoint summaries
      starts_with("mean_log2_"),
      # Per-sample log2 values
      all_of(log2_cols),
      # Statistics
      all_of(stat_cols),
      # Annotations
      go_molecular_function, go_biological_process,
      go_cellular_component, kegg_pathway
    )
  
  write_csv(prot_clean, output_name, na = "")
  message("✔ Wrote ", output_name, " (", nrow(prot_clean), " rows)")
  
  return(prot_clean)
}

# Apply to both datasets
prot_cellular_clean <- clean_proteomics(prot_raw_cellular, "cellular_proteomics_clean_v2.csv")
prot_soluble_clean <- clean_proteomics(prot_raw_soluble, "soluble_proteomics_clean_v2.csv")


# REFORMAT INSOLUBLE FRACTION, data was in a excel file instead of txt
# Converts InsolubleFraction_Astral.xlsx into the same structure as cellular_proteomics_clean_v2.csv and soluble_proteomics_clean_v2.csv

# INPUT:  InsolubleFraction_Astral.xlsx
# OUTPUT: insoluble_proteomics_clean_v2.csv

options(stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

library(readxl)    # read Excel files
library(dplyr)     # data manipulation
library(tidyr)     # reshaping


# 1. READ THE INSOLUBLE EXCEL FILE
# Skip the first row (it contains a merged header "log2 normalized abundance...")
# The actual column names are on row 2


insol_raw <- read_excel(
  "results/tables/InsolubleFraction_Astral.xlsx",
  sheet = 1,
  skip  = 1    # skip the merged header row; row 2 becomes the column names
)

cat("Raw dimensions:", nrow(insol_raw), "rows x", ncol(insol_raw), "cols\n")
cat("Column names:\n")
print(names(insol_raw))


# 2. RENAME IDENTIFIER COLUMNS to match cellular/soluble
# Raw names: PG.ProteinAccessions, Gene Name, PG.ProteinDescriptions, PG.ProteinNames
# Target:    uniprot_id,            gene_symbol, protein_description,  protein_groups


insol <- insol_raw %>%
  rename(
    uniprot_id          = PG.ProteinAccessions,
    gene_symbol         = `Gene Name`,
    protein_description = PG.ProteinDescriptions,
    protein_groups      = PG.ProteinNames       # closest equivalent to protein_groups
  )


# 3. IDENTIFY SAMPLE COLUMNS AND MAP TO TIMEPOINTS
# Column name pattern: "[N] PR807_MS2_XC_YYD/M_Astral.htrms.PG.Quantity"
# Timepoint codes in raw data: 14D, 21D, 3M, 6M, 12M, 18M
# Target timepoint labels:     D14, D21, M03, M06, M12, M18
# All quantity columns (everything except the 4 identifier columns)
id_cols      <- c("uniprot_id", "gene_symbol", "protein_description", "protein_groups")
sample_cols  <- setdiff(names(insol), id_cols)

# Function to extract timepoint label from raw column name
get_timepoint <- function(col) {
  dplyr::case_when(
    grepl("14D",  col) ~ "D14",
    grepl("21D",  col) ~ "D21",
    grepl("_3M_", col) ~ "M03",
    grepl("_6M_", col) ~ "M06",
    grepl("12M",  col) ~ "M12",
    grepl("18M",  col) ~ "M18",
    TRUE               ~ NA_character_
  )
}

# Map each sample column to its timepoint
col_tp_map <- data.frame(
  raw_col   = sample_cols,
  timepoint = vapply(sample_cols, get_timepoint, character(1)),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

cat("\nTimepoint mapping:\n")
print(col_tp_map)

# Verify all columns were mapped
if (any(is.na(col_tp_map$timepoint))) {
  warning("Some columns could not be mapped to a timepoint — check col_tp_map")
}

# 4. RENAME SAMPLE COLUMNS to match cellular/soluble format:
# e.g. "D14.20713.Quantity.log2"
# Since insoluble sample IDs differ, we use rep index (1-5) per timepoint

tp_levels <- c("D14", "D21", "M03", "M06", "M12", "M18")

# Build new column names: timepoint.repN.Quantity.log2
col_tp_map <- col_tp_map %>%
  group_by(timepoint) %>%
  mutate(rep_n    = row_number(),
         new_col  = paste0(timepoint, ".rep", rep_n, ".Quantity.log2")) %>%
  ungroup()

# Rename the columns in the dataframe
rename_vec <- setNames(col_tp_map$raw_col, col_tp_map$new_col)
insol      <- insol %>% rename(all_of(rename_vec))

cat("\nRenamed sample columns:\n")
print(names(insol))


# 5. COMPUTE MEAN LOG2 INTENSITY PER TIMEPOINT
# Matches columns like mean_log2_D14, mean_log2_M03 etc. in cellular/soluble

get_tp_cols <- function(df, tp) {
  grep(paste0("^", tp, "\\.rep"), names(df), value = TRUE)
}

for (tp in tp_levels) {
  cols <- get_tp_cols(insol, tp)
  if (length(cols) > 0) {
    col_name        <- paste0("mean_log2_", tp)
    insol[[col_name]] <- rowMeans(insol[, cols, drop = FALSE], na.rm = TRUE)
  }
}


# 6. COMPUTE DETECTION METRICS
# n_samples_detected : number of samples where the protein was detected (non-NA)
# pct_samples_detected: percentage of all samples with detection


all_sample_cols <- col_tp_map$new_col
n_total_samples <- length(all_sample_cols)

insol$n_samples_detected  <- rowSums(!is.na(insol[, all_sample_cols]))
insol$pct_samples_detected <- round(
  100 * insol$n_samples_detected / n_total_samples, 2
)

# 7. ARRANGE COLUMNS IN SAME ORDER AS CELLULAR/SOLUBLE
# id cols | QC cols | mean_log2 per tp | sample Quantity.log2 cols
# Note: mol_weight_da, n_precursors_exp, median_precursors_used not available
#       in the insoluble file — filled with NA for compatibility

# Ordered mean log2 columns
mean_cols <- paste0("mean_log2_", tp_levels)
mean_cols <- mean_cols[mean_cols %in% names(insol)]

# Ordered sample columns (by timepoint then replicate)
ordered_sample_cols <- col_tp_map %>%
  arrange(match(timepoint, tp_levels), rep_n) %>%
  pull(new_col)

# Add placeholder columns that exist in cellular/soluble but not insoluble
insol$mol_weight_da        <- NA_real_
insol$n_precursors_exp     <- NA_integer_
insol$median_precursors_used <- NA_real_

# Placeholder annotation columns
insol$go_molecular_function <- NA_character_
insol$go_biological_process <- NA_character_
insol$go_cellular_component <- NA_character_
insol$kegg_pathway          <- NA_character_

# Assemble in final order
final_col_order <- c(
  "gene_symbol",
  "uniprot_id",
  "protein_groups",
  "protein_description",
  "mol_weight_da",
  "n_precursors_exp",
  "n_samples_detected",
  "pct_samples_detected",
  "median_precursors_used",
  mean_cols,
  ordered_sample_cols,
  "go_molecular_function",
  "go_biological_process",
  "go_cellular_component",
  "kegg_pathway"
)

# Keep only columns that exist
final_col_order <- final_col_order[final_col_order %in% names(insol)]
insol_final     <- insol[, final_col_order]

write.csv(insol_final, "insoluble_proteomics_clean_v2.csv", row.names = FALSE)

cat("\nDone! Output saved to: insoluble_proteomics_clean_v2.csv\n")
cat("Dimensions:", nrow(insol_final), "proteins x", ncol(insol_final), "columns\n")
cat("\nColumn names in output:\n")
print(names(insol_final))
```


```{r}
# Implementing Batch Correction via ComBat
# BATCH CORRECTION (ComBat)
# Applies empirical Bayes batch correction across the three subcellular
# fractions, treating fraction/instrument as the batch variable.
#


library(sva)
library(tidyverse)

# 1. Load the three cleaned datasets
cellular  <- read.csv("cellular_proteomics_clean_v2.csv")
soluble   <- read.csv("soluble_proteomics_clean_v2.csv")
insoluble <- read.csv("insoluble_proteomics_clean_v2.csv")

tp_levels <- c("D14", "D21", "M03", "M06", "M12", "M18")
mean_cols <- paste0("mean_log2_", tp_levels)

# 2. Identify proteins shared across all three fractions
shared_genes <- Reduce(intersect, list(
  cellular$gene_symbol,
  soluble$gene_symbol,
  insoluble$gene_symbol
))

cat("Shared genes across all three fractions:", length(shared_genes), "\n")

# 3. Build a combined expression matrix (mean log2 per timepoint)
build_shared_matrix <- function(df, prefix, genes) {
  df %>%
    filter(gene_symbol %in% genes) %>%
    distinct(gene_symbol, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
    arrange(gene_symbol) %>%
    select(gene_symbol, all_of(mean_cols)) %>%
    column_to_rownames("gene_symbol") %>%
    rename_with(~ paste0(prefix, "_", .x))
}

mat_cellular  <- build_shared_matrix(cellular,  "Cellular",  shared_genes)
mat_soluble   <- build_shared_matrix(soluble,   "Soluble",   shared_genes)
mat_insoluble <- build_shared_matrix(insoluble, "Insoluble", shared_genes)

combined_mat <- cbind(mat_cellular, mat_soluble, mat_insoluble) %>%
  as.matrix()

# Restrict to complete cases (required by ComBat)
combined_mat <- combined_mat[complete.cases(combined_mat), ]

cat("Genes retained after complete-case filtering:", nrow(combined_mat), "\n")

# 4. Define batch (acquisition instrument, matching fraction identity) ─
batch <- c(
  rep("Exploris480_cellular", ncol(mat_cellular)),
  rep("Exploris480_soluble",  ncol(mat_soluble)),
  rep("Astral_insoluble",     ncol(mat_insoluble))
)

# 5. Run ComBat
combat_mat <- ComBat(dat = combined_mat, batch = batch)

# 6. Split corrected matrix back into per-fraction data frames
n_cell <- ncol(mat_cellular)
n_sol  <- ncol(mat_soluble)
n_insol <- ncol(mat_insoluble)

cellular_corrected  <- combat_mat[, 1:n_cell] %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("gene_symbol") %>%
  rename_with(~ str_remove(.x, "^Cellular_"), -gene_symbol)

soluble_corrected   <- combat_mat[, (n_cell + 1):(n_cell + n_sol)] %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("gene_symbol") %>%
  rename_with(~ str_remove(.x, "^Soluble_"), -gene_symbol)

insoluble_corrected <- combat_mat[, (n_cell + n_sol + 1):(n_cell + n_sol + n_insol)] %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("gene_symbol") %>%
  rename_with(~ str_remove(.x, "^Insoluble_"), -gene_symbol)

# 7. Export corrected values for the shared protein set
write.csv(cellular_corrected,  "cellular_shared_proteins_combat_corrected.csv",  row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(soluble_corrected,   "soluble_shared_proteins_combat_corrected.csv",   row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(insoluble_corrected, "insoluble_shared_proteins_combat_corrected.csv", row.names = FALSE)

cat("\n✔ ComBat correction complete.\n")
cat("Corrected values exported for", nrow(combat_mat), "shared proteins per fraction.\n")
cat("NOTE: fraction-exclusive proteins (not detected in all three fractions)\n")
cat("      remain uncorrected in the original _clean_v2.csv files, since\n")
cat("      ComBat cannot estimate batch effects without cross-batch data.\n")
```


```{r}
# PLOTTING BATCH CORREECTION PCA AND VISUALISING PROTEIN ABUNDANCES ACROSS FRACTIONS
library(tidyverse)
library(sva)        # for ComBat
library(patchwork)

# 0. Load cleaned proteomics data

cellular  <- read.csv("cellular_proteomics_clean_v2.csv")
soluble   <- read.csv("soluble_proteomics_clean_v2.csv")
insoluble <- read.csv("insoluble_proteomics_clean_v2.csv")


# 1. Total proteins identified per fraction

protein_counts <- tibble(
  Fraction = c("Cellular", "Soluble", "Insoluble"),
  Total_Proteins = c(nrow(cellular), nrow(soluble), nrow(insoluble))
)
print(protein_counts)


# 2. Prep shared proteins across fractions for ComBat demonstration

prep_fraction <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    filter(!is.na(gene_symbol)) %>%
    select(gene_symbol, matches("\\.Quantity\\.log2$")) %>%
    group_by(gene_symbol) %>%
    summarise(across(everything(), ~mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE)), .groups = "drop")
}

cell_mat <- prep_fraction(cellular)
sol_mat  <- prep_fraction(soluble)
ins_mat  <- prep_fraction(insoluble)

shared <- cell_mat %>%
  inner_join(sol_mat, by = "gene_symbol", suffix = c(".cell", ".sol")) %>%
  inner_join(ins_mat, by = "gene_symbol", suffix = c("", ".ins"))

cat("\nShared proteins across all three fractions:", nrow(shared), "\n")

expr_mat <- shared %>% select(-gene_symbol) %>% as.matrix()
rownames(expr_mat) <- shared$gene_symbol

expr_mat_complete <- expr_mat[complete.cases(expr_mat), ]
cat("Complete-case proteins used for PCA/ComBat:", nrow(expr_mat_complete), "\n")

batch <- c(rep("Exploris480_cellular", ncol(cell_mat) - 1),
           rep("Exploris480_soluble",  ncol(sol_mat) - 1),
           rep("Astral_insoluble",     ncol(ins_mat) - 1))


# 3. PCA before and after ComBat

pca_before <- prcomp(t(expr_mat_complete), scale. = TRUE)
pca_before_df <- as.data.frame(pca_before$x[, 1:2]) %>%
  mutate(Batch = batch, Stage = "Before ComBat")

expr_corrected <- ComBat(dat = expr_mat_complete, batch = batch, par.prior = TRUE)

pca_after <- prcomp(t(expr_corrected), scale. = TRUE)
pca_after_df <- as.data.frame(pca_after$x[, 1:2]) %>%
  mutate(Batch = batch, Stage = "After ComBat")

pca_combined <- bind_rows(pca_before_df, pca_after_df) %>%
  mutate(Stage = factor(Stage, levels = c("Before ComBat", "After ComBat")))

# =====================================================================
# 4. Colours (Okabe-Ito, colourblind-safe)
# =====================================================================
fraction_colours <- c(
  "Cellular"  = "#0072B2",
  "Soluble"   = "#E69F00",
  "Insoluble" = "#009E73"
)

batch_colours <- c(
  "Exploris480_cellular" = "#0072B2",
  "Exploris480_soluble"  = "#E69F00",
  "Astral_insoluble"     = "#009E73"
)


# 5. Panel A: total protein counts per fraction

p_counts <- ggplot(protein_counts, aes(x = Fraction, y = Total_Proteins, fill = Fraction)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Total_Proteins), vjust = -0.6, size = 4.5, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fraction_colours) +
  labs(title = "Total proteins identified per fraction",
       x = "", y = "Number of proteins identified") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, max(protein_counts$Total_Proteins) * 1.15))


# 6. Panel B: PCA before/after ComBat as two SEPARATE plots

shared_lims <- range(c(pca_combined$PC1, pca_combined$PC2))

p_before <- ggplot(pca_before_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, colour = Batch)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = batch_colours) +
  coord_equal(xlim = shared_lims, ylim = shared_lims) +
  labs(title = "Before ComBat", x = "PC1", y = "PC2") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))

p_after <- ggplot(pca_after_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, colour = Batch)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = batch_colours) +
  coord_equal(xlim = shared_lims, ylim = shared_lims) +
  labs(title = "After ComBat", x = "PC1", y = "PC2") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))

p_combat <- (p_before | p_after) +
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") +
  plot_annotation(title = "PCA before and after ComBat batch correction",
                   subtitle = "Shared proteins across cellular, soluble, and insoluble fractions") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))


# 7. Combine Panel A + Panel B into final figure

final_figure <- (p_counts | p_combat) +
  plot_layout(widths = c(1, 2)) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = "A") &
  theme(plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16))

final_figure

ggsave("protein_detection_overview_patchwork.pdf", final_figure, width = 14, height = 6)
ggsave("protein_detection_overview_patchwork.png", final_figure, width = 14, height = 6, dpi = 300, bg = "white")
```
